rulings.Case Title: TERRA NOVA INDUSTRIES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. M/M WINE REALTY, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 11, 2016, line 3, DEFENDANT PAOLO MANCINI's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for specific performance for Plaintiff to allege that defendant failed to comply with the demand made under the agreement per SAC 5:26-27. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Estoppel is sufficiently alleged re: Statute of Limitations and Statute of Frauds. Fraud is sufficiently alleged. = (302/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID A. BRADLOW VS. NATALIE MARDEUSZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Under California Code Of Civil Procedure 425.06, The Anit-Slapp Statutes
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 11, 2016, line 8, DEFENDANT FRANK MARDEUSZ' Motion To Strike Under California Code Of Civil Procedure 425.06, The Anit-Slapp Statutes DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden under CCP 425.16 and show that this action arises out of protected activity. "In the anti-SLAPP context, the critical point is whether the plaintiff's cause of action itself was based on an act in furtherance of the defendant's right of petition or free speech." City of Cotati v. Cashman (2002) 29 Cal.4th 69, 78. The plaintiff' cause of action here is based on Defendants' failure to vacate after expiration of the Notice to Terminate, not a protected activity under CCP 425.16(e). = (302/REQ)
